utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format contains 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all five of these items. Each one is specified with relevant details and costs where required: health_coverage includes detailed physical and mental health coverage plus a monthly cost; property_coverage includes features and a coverage limit (though cost is not explicitly broken out, it is implicitly part of the overall cost); travel_coverage includes features and a coverage limit (same note on cost); additional_services lists features (with no explicit cost but covered under total_budget); total_budget provides complete monthly and annual cost breakdown. Since all five items appear and are reasonably detailed with the expected information, they are all considered successfully included.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists the main insurance bundle as 'LifeFirst Active Bundle0'. This appears to be a composite plan combining LifeFirst-branded health, property, and travel coverages. To map ratings, we match each coverage type to the most relevant LifeFirst option in the ground-truth ratings. For health_coverage, both physical_health and mental_health correspond to LifeFirst Standard Plan (rating 9 for both). For property_coverage, the equivalent is LifeFirst Standard Coverage (rating 9). For travel_coverage, the matching plan is LifeFirst Standard Plan (rating 9). The additional_services section includes 24/7 global assistance, concierge, dedicated agent, and mobile app portal access. These align most closely with Premium Insurance Membership (rating 6) under general_services. Therefore, the final mapping is as follows.
